title: Iran allows two French former detainees to leave the country, Macron says
content: Macron has distanced France from the war in Iran, saying his country wasn't consulted in advance about the US-Israel strikes and didn’t want the war.
snippet: Macron has distanced France from the war in Iran, saying his country wasn't consulted in advance about the US-Israel strikes and didn’t want the war.

title: How serious will the jet fuel crisis in Europe become?
content: Jet fuel prices have increased by 95% since the United States and Israel launched military attacks against Iran on 28 February. Higher fares, fuel surcharges and reductions in capacity or limiting of unprofitable routes will be the new normal, energy analysts warn.

title: Why did Italy deny US bombers access to an Italian airbase?
content: Claims circulating online suggest Italy denied US military aircraft access to the Sigonella base in Sicily for political reasons linked to the war in Iran. However, official statements and reporting from multiple sources indicate the situation is more procedural than political.

title: US Vice President Vance attacks Brussels and vows to help Orbán ahead of Hungarian vote
content: Vance accused the European Union of electoral interference in Hungary’s election campaign during a visit to Budapest. Speaking alongside Orbán, Vance described the Hungarian leader as a “role model” for Europe.

title: Don't commit war crimes in Iran, EU urges Trump as deadline looms
content: Donald Trump's threat to strike Iran's bridges and power plants has raised the prospect of war crimes. The EU insists all sides should respect international law and exercise "maximum restraint".
snippet: Donald Trump's threat to strike Iran's bridges and power plants has raised the prospect of war crimes. The EU insists all sides should respect international law and exercise "maximum restraint".

title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
content: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

title: Summer holidays: Why travellers are swapping the Riviera for the Balkans
content: Many tend to think of France, Greece, Italy and Spain when booking their ideal European summer destination. But new figures suggest there's a new country on the scene.
snippet: Many tend to think of France, Greece, Italy and Spain when booking their ideal European summer destination. But new figures suggest there's a new country on the scene.

title: Hundreds of motorcyclists join Portuguese priest in Easter celebration on wheels
content: Hundreds of bikers joined Father Avelino in Sintra for the 19th Easter motorcycle procession, blending Catholic tradition with roaring engines.
snippet: Hundreds of bikers joined Father Avelino in Sintra for the 19th Easter motorcycle procession, blending Catholic tradition with roaring engines.

title: Pro-Orbán actors accuse Meta of interference in Hungarian elections, despite lack of evidence
content: Meta has found itself embroiled in Hungary's tense election campaign, after supporters of the ruling Fidesz party claimed their posts are being restricted to favour opposition parties. In reality, there is no weight to the allegations.

title: Jet fuel crisis: Rationing triggered at four airports in Italy
content: Four northern Italian airports introduce restrictions on jet fuel due to the energy crisis, with priority given to long-haul and medical flights. Meanwhile, Ryanair has warned that if the Iran war continues, summer flights are at risk across Europe.

title: Explosives found near gas pipeline connecting Serbia and Hungary, Vučić says
content: Serbian president Aleksandar Vučić has said that explosives were found by police near a pipeline that carries gas from Russia to Serbia and Hungary, but did not elaborate on potential suspects or their motives.

title: Zelenskyy warns US-Iran war could divert critical aid from Ukraine
content: President Zelenskyy has warned that a prolonged US-Israeli war with Iran is threatening the supply of vital Patriot missiles and boosting Russia’s economy through surging oil prices.
snippet: President Zelenskyy has warned that a prolonged US-Israeli war with Iran is threatening the supply of vital Patriot missiles and boosting Russia’s economy through surging oil prices.

title: Sea-Watch rescue 44 migrants from abandoned oil rig in Mediterranean
content: Dozens of migrants have been rescued from an abandoned Mediterranean oil rig. It is the second incident of its kind this week, highlighting the dangers of the North Africa crossing.
snippet: Dozens of migrants have been rescued from an abandoned Mediterranean oil rig. It is the second incident of its kind this week, highlighting the dangers of the North Africa crossing.

title: Camorra boss Roberto Mazzarella caught in luxury resort on Amalfi coast
content: Mazzarella, head of the clan bearing his name, was on the list of Italy's most dangerous fugitives. Murder charges and other charges were brought against him. Cash and luxury watches were seized in the operation.

title: Five EU ministers call for new windfall tax on energy profits amid price surge
content: Economy and finance ministers from Italy, Germany, Spain, Portugal and Austria are asking the EU for a tax on energy surplus profits to curb price increases.

title: Fact check: How can a country actually withdraw from NATO?
content: Donald Trump has threatened to pull out of the North Atlantic alliance over European countries' reticence to join the war in Iran. However, questions remain over how exactly he would be able to do so, both under domestic law and NATO treaties.

title: Dangerous lignite: Bosnia and Herzegovina has problems with its energy transition
content: Will Bosnia-Herzegovina's belated start to the energy transition delay its EU accession? The country is struggling to move away from coal-fired power generation. There is a shortage of desulphurisation plants. The air pollution is at an extreme level. The consequences are serious: Cancer from coal?

title: How long can the EU’s oil reserves last?
content: The 400 million barrels of oil released by the International Energy Agency — of which 92 million barrels were contributed by 20 EU countries — could last about five months, according to analysts. The bloc also relies on storage reserves.

title: Which European countries have the best salaries after taxes?
content: Social contributions can weigh as much as a third of the salary paid to an employee.

title: Passerelles, Article 7, coffee breaks: How the EU works around the veto
content: The EU’s unanimity rule lets any single member state veto decisions, turning consensus into a power play. Now, the bloc is pushing back with whatever tools it has.
snippet: The EU’s unanimity rule lets any single member state veto decisions, turning consensus into a power play. Now, the bloc is pushing back with whatever tools it has.

title: Hungarian opposition Tisza party cements lead ahead of April elections, polls show
content: Set of polls indicate that Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party is ahead of Viktor Orbán’s Fidesz by 19–23 points among likely voters. Hungarians head to the polls in Europe's most consequential election on 12 April.

title: EU readies first payment to Ukraine as soon as Hungary lifts veto on €90bn loan
content: The first payment to Ukraine under the €90 billion loan could happen in a matter of days after Hungary lifts its veto. EU officials, however, concede the dispute will be solved only after the country goes to the polls on 12 April.

title: Can AGILE boost the EU’s defence readiness? Ask the Euronews chatbot

title: Podcast: Everything you need to know about the Hungarian elections

title: US Vice President Vance to visit Hungary days before key election
